subtitle.en: Two ways to wire login on Cloudflare: Zero Trust Access, or your own sessions in KV
subtitle.zh: 在 Cloudflare 串接登入的兩種做法:零信任 Access,或自己用 KV 管 session

tagline.en: From a no-code login wall to a hand-built session cookie — see exactly how auth flows across the stack.
tagline.zh: 從免寫程式的登入牆,到手刻的 session cookie —— 看清認證如何在整個技術棧裡流動。

text.en: "Authentication" just means proving who a user is before letting them in. On Cloudflare you have two clean ways to do it, and this guide walks through both so you can pick the right one.
text.zh: 「認證(Authentication)」其實就是:在放使用者進來之前,先確認他是誰。在 Cloudflare 上有兩種乾淨的做法,本篇會帶你走過兩種,讓你挑對適合自己的那一種。

text.en: Approach A is Cloudflare Access (Zero Trust): identity sits in front of your whole app and you write zero code. Approach B is app-level sessions: your own Worker checks the password, stores a session in Workers KV, and hands the browser a cookie.
text.zh: 做法 A 是 Cloudflare Access(零信任):身分驗證直接擋在整個應用程式前面,你完全不用寫程式。做法 B 是應用程式層級的 session:你自己的 Worker 檢查密碼、把 session 存進 Workers KV,再發一個 cookie 給瀏覽器。

title.en: The two paths at a glance

code.en:
flowchart LR
  U["Browser"] --> Choice{"Which auth?"}
  Choice -->|"Zero Trust"| CA["Cloudflare Access"]
  Choice -->|"App login"| W["Your Worker"]
  CA --> App["Protected app"]
  W --> KV["Workers KV sessions"]
  W --> App
code.zh:
flowchart LR
  U["瀏覽器"] --> Choice{"用哪種認證?"}
  Choice -->|"零信任"| CA["Cloudflare Access"]
  Choice -->|"應用程式登入"| W["你的 Worker"]
  CA --> App["受保護的應用程式"]
  W --> KV["Workers KV session"]
  W --> App

text.en: Identity check in front of the app. No code, managed in the Zero Trust dashboard. Great for internal tools.
text.zh: 在應用程式前面做身分檢查。免寫程式,在 Zero Trust 儀表板設定。很適合內部工具。

text.en: Your Worker verifies the login, stores a session in KV, and sets a cookie. Full control, needs code. Great for public apps.
text.zh: 你的 Worker 驗證登入、把 session 存進 KV、再設一個 cookie。完全掌控、但要寫程式。很適合公開的應用程式。

text.en: Access is hiring a security guard for the whole building's front door. Building your own session is giving each visitor a numbered wristband at your own reception desk and checking that band at every room.
text.zh: Access 就像幫整棟大樓的大門請一位保全。自建 session 則是你在自家櫃檯給每位訪客一條有編號的手環,之後每進一個房間都核對那條手環。

heading.zh: 做法 A — Cloudflare Access(免寫程式)

text.en: Cloudflare Access puts a login wall in front of your app at the edge. The user never reaches your app until Access has confirmed their identity with a provider you trust (Google, GitHub, Microsoft Entra ID, or a one-time email PIN).
text.zh: Cloudflare Access 在邊緣(edge)幫你的應用程式擋一道登入牆。在 Access 用你信任的提供者(Google、GitHub、Microsoft Entra ID,或寄到 email 的一次性密碼)確認身分之前,使用者根本碰不到你的應用程式。

text.en: This is Single Sign-On (SSO): the user logs in once with their existing account and Access issues a signed token (a JWT) as a cookie. Your app can stay completely unaware of passwords.
text.zh: 這就是單一登入(SSO,Single Sign-On):使用者用既有帳號登入一次,Access 就發一個已簽章的權杖(JWT)當作 cookie。你的應用程式可以完全不用碰密碼。

code.en:
sequenceDiagram
  participant U as "Browser"
  participant CA as "Cloudflare Access"
  participant IdP as "Identity Provider"
  participant App as "Your App"
  U->>CA: Open the app URL
  CA-->>U: Redirect to login
  U->>IdP: Sign in with Google
  IdP-->>CA: Identity confirmed
  CA->>CA: Check Access policy
  CA-->>U: Set signed JWT cookie
  U->>App: Request carries the JWT
  App-->>U: Protected page
code.zh:
sequenceDiagram
  participant U as "瀏覽器"
  participant CA as "Cloudflare Access"
  participant IdP as "身分提供者"
  participant App as "你的應用程式"
  U->>CA: 打開應用程式網址
  CA-->>U: 轉址到登入頁
  U->>IdP: 用 Google 登入
  IdP-->>CA: 身分確認完成
  CA->>CA: 檢查 Access 政策
  CA-->>U: 設定已簽章的 JWT cookie
  U->>App: 請求帶著 JWT
  App-->>U: 受保護的頁面

text.en: Add the app and a policy in the dashboard. Nothing to write, build, or deploy.
text.zh: 在儀表板新增應用程式和一條政策即可。不用寫、不用建置、也不用部署。

text.en: Plug in Google Workspace, GitHub, or Entra ID so people use accounts they already have.
text.zh: 接上 Google Workspace、GitHub 或 Entra ID,大家直接用既有帳號。

text.en: Allow only @yourcompany.com emails, require MFA, or restrict by group.
text.zh: 只允許 @yourcompany.com 的 email、要求多因素驗證,或依群組限制。

text.en: Every login attempt is recorded — who, what, and when — with no extra work.
text.zh: 每次登入嘗試都會被記錄(誰、做了什麼、何時),不用額外做任何事。

title.en: Your app can still trust the token
title.zh: 你的應用程式仍可信任這個權杖

text.en: Access forwards a signed Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion header. If you want, your Worker can verify that JWT against Cloudflare's public keys to read the user's email — but you never have to handle the login itself.
text.zh: Access 會轉發一個已簽章的 Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion 標頭。需要的話,你的 Worker 可以拿 Cloudflare 的公鑰驗證這個 JWT 來讀出使用者 email —— 但你完全不必自己處理登入。

heading.en: Approach B — App-level sessions in KV
heading.zh: 做法 B — 應用程式層級的 KV session

text.en: When you run your own sign-up and login (public app, custom UI, your own user table), you handle auth in code. The classic, robust pattern is the server-side session backed by a cookie.
text.zh: 當你要自己做註冊和登入(公開的應用程式、自訂介面、你自己的使用者資料表)時,認證就由程式碼處理。最經典、最穩健的模式就是「伺服器端 session + cookie」。

text.en: The Worker checks the password, creates a random session id, stores the real session data in Workers KV with an expiry, and sends the browser a cookie holding only that id. On later requests the browser auto-sends the cookie and the Worker looks the session up in KV.
text.zh: Worker 檢查密碼、產生一個隨機的 session id、把真正的 session 資料連同到期時間存進 Workers KV,然後發給瀏覽器一個「只裝這個 id」的 cookie。之後每次請求,瀏覽器會自動帶上 cookie,Worker 再用它去 KV 查出 session。

code.en:
sequenceDiagram
  participant U as "Browser"
  participant W as "Worker"
  participant KV as "Workers KV"
  U->>W: POST /login email and password
  W->>W: Verify the password hash
  W->>KV: put session id and user json
  KV-->>W: stored with a TTL
  W-->>U: Set-Cookie session id
  U->>W: GET /me with the cookie
  W->>KV: get session id
  KV-->>W: user json
  W-->>U: Profile JSON
code.zh:
sequenceDiagram
  participant U as "瀏覽器"
  participant W as "Worker"
  participant KV as "Workers KV"
  U->>W: POST /login 帶 email 與密碼
  W->>W: 驗證密碼雜湊
  W->>KV: put 寫入 session id 與使用者 json
  KV-->>W: 已存入並設定 TTL
  W-->>U: Set-Cookie 回傳 session id
  U->>W: GET /me 帶著 cookie
  W->>KV: get 查 session id
  KV-->>W: 使用者 json
  W-->>U: 個人資料 JSON

text.en: The cookie holds a random id only. User data lives server-side in KV, never in the browser.
text.zh: cookie 只裝一個隨機 id。使用者資料放在伺服器端的 KV,絕不放在瀏覽器裡。

text.en: expirationTtl makes KV delete the session automatically — logout-by-timeout for free.
text.zh: expirationTtl 會讓 KV 自動刪掉 session —— 等於免費的「逾時自動登出」。

text.en: Delete the KV key and that session is dead immediately, even before it expires.
text.zh: 刪掉那個 KV key,該 session 立刻失效,就算還沒到期也一樣。

text.en: Your routes, your cookie flags, your rules. You can add roles, refresh, anything.
text.zh: 你的路由、你的 cookie 旗標、你的規則。可以加角色、加更新機制,什麼都行。

title.zh: 為什麼 KV 適合存 session

text.en: Sessions are read on almost every request and KV is built for read-heavy, globally-replicated lookups with a built-in TTL. That makes it a natural home for session data on Workers.
text.zh: 幾乎每個請求都要讀 session,而 KV 正是為「讀多、全球同步、內建 TTL」的查詢而設計的,所以拿來放 Workers 上的 session 資料剛剛好。

text.en: Pick by audience. If you are gating an internal tool or staff dashboard, Access wins — it's faster and safer than anything you'd hand-roll. If you are building a public product where strangers sign up, you need your own sessions.
text.zh: 依「對象」來選。如果你要把關的是內部工具或員工後台,Access 勝出 —— 它比你自己手刻的任何東西都更快也更安全。如果你在做一個對外、讓陌生人自行註冊的產品,那你就需要自己的 session。

title.zh: 決策:Access 還是自建 session

code.en:
flowchart TD
  Start["Need to protect something"] --> Q1{"Internal tool or staff app?"}
  Q1 -->|Yes| Access["Use Cloudflare Access"]
  Q1 -->|No| Q2{"Public app with user accounts?"}
  Q2 -->|Yes| Build["Build sessions in KV"]
  Q2 -->|No| Q3{"Just block bots on a form?"}
  Q3 -->|Yes| Turnstile["Add Turnstile"]
  Q3 -->|No| Access
  Access --> Done["Login wall ready"]
  Build --> Done
  Turnstile --> Done
code.zh:
flowchart TD
  Start["需要保護某個東西"] --> Q1{"內部工具或員工系統?"}
  Q1 -->|是| Access["用 Cloudflare Access"]
  Q1 -->|否| Q2{"公開且有使用者帳號?"}
  Q2 -->|是| Build["自建 KV session"]
  Q2 -->|否| Q3{"只是想擋表單機器人?"}
  Q3 -->|是| Turnstile["加上 Turnstile"]
  Q3 -->|否| Access
  Access --> Done["登入牆完成"]
  Build --> Done
  Turnstile --> Done

text.en: Internal apps, admin panels, staging sites, SSH/RDP, or anything for a known set of people.
text.zh: 內部應用程式、管理後台、測試站、SSH／遠端桌面,或任何給「已知一群人」用的東西。

text.en: Public sign-up, custom login UI, social accounts, per-user data, or a mobile/SPA backend.
text.zh: 對外開放註冊、自訂登入介面、社群帳號、每位使用者各自的資料,或行動／SPA 後端。

text.en: If you only need to stop bots on a contact or signup form, add Turnstile and skip auth entirely.
text.zh: 如果你只是要擋住聯絡或註冊表單上的機器人,加個 Turnstile 就好,完全不用做認證。

text.en: It's common to use Access for the admin area and your own KV sessions for end-user accounts in the same project. They don't conflict.
text.zh: 同一個專案裡,用 Access 守後台、用自己的 KV session 處理一般使用者帳號,是很常見的組合。兩者並不衝突。

heading.en: Build it: a session Worker end to end
heading.zh: 動手做:一個完整的 session Worker

text.en: Here is approach B in full: create the KV namespaces, bind them, seed a demo user, then the Worker (login + middleware + logout) and a tiny front-end. Approach A needs no code — just the dashboard steps from the Access section.
text.zh: 以下是做法 B 的完整版:建立 KV 命名空間、綁定、塞一個示範使用者,接著是 Worker(登入 + 中介層 + 登出)和一個小小的前端。做法 A 不用寫程式,照前面 Access 那段的儀表板步驟做即可。

title.zh: 建立兩個 KV 命名空間

text.en: One stores sessions, one stores the demo users. Wrangler prints an id for each.
text.zh: 一個存 session,一個存示範使用者。Wrangler 會各印出一個 id。

code.body:
# Two namespaces: one for sessions, one for the demo user store
npx wrangler kv namespace create SESSIONS
npx wrangler kv namespace create USERS

text.en: Paste the ids so the Worker can reach them as env.SESSIONS and env.USERS.
text.zh: 把 id 貼進來,Worker 就能用 env.SESSIONS 和 env.USERS 取用。

text.en: Store one user whose passwordHash is the SHA-256 of the password "hunter2".
text.zh: 存一位使用者,他的 passwordHash 就是密碼「hunter2」的 SHA-256。

code.body:
# Seed one demo user. passwordHash below is SHA-256 of "hunter2".
npx wrangler kv key put --binding USERS \
  "user:alice@example.com" \
  '{"email":"alice@example.com","passwordHash":"f52fbd32b2b3b86ff88ef6c490628285f482af15ddcb29541f94bcf526a3f6c7"}' \
  --remote

text.en: The Worker: login, middleware, logout
text.zh: Worker:登入、中介層、登出

text.en: Note the password check uses SHA-256 only to keep the demo short. In production, hash passwords with a real KDF (key derivation function) such as Argon2id, scrypt, or bcrypt, or PBKDF2 via Web Crypto with a high iteration count and a per-user salt.
text.zh: 注意:這裡密碼檢查只用 SHA-256 是為了讓示範簡短。正式環境請改用真正的 KDF(金鑰衍生函式)來雜湊密碼,例如 Argon2id、scrypt、bcrypt,或用 Web Crypto 的 PBKDF2 搭配高迭代次數與每位使用者各自的鹽(salt)。

text.en: The front-end: a login form
text.zh: 前端:登入表單

text.en: credentials: "include" is the key detail — it tells the browser to store the Set-Cookie response and resend the cookie on later requests, so /me just works.
text.zh: credentials: "include" 是關鍵 —— 它叫瀏覽器存下回應的 Set-Cookie,並在之後的請求自動帶上 cookie,這樣 /me 才會直接通。

blocks.body:
<!-- A plain login form posting JSON to the Worker -->
<form id="login">
  <input name="email" type="email" placeholder="Email" required />
  <input name="password" type="password" placeholder="Password" required />
  <button>Log in</button>
</form>

<script>
  const form = document.getElementById("login");
  form.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    const res = await fetch("/login", {
      method: "POST",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      credentials: "include", // let the browser store & resend the cookie
      body: JSON.stringify({
        email: form.email.value,
        password: form.password.value,
      }),
    });
    if (res.ok) {
      // The session cookie is set; protected fetches now work automatically.
      const me = await fetch("/me", { credentials: "include" }).then((r) => r.json());
      alert(`Welcome, ${me.email}`);
    } else {
      alert("Login failed");
    }
  });
</script>

text.en: Always serve over HTTPS (the Secure flag needs it), keep cookies HttpOnly so JavaScript can't read them, use a real password KDF, and add rate limiting plus Turnstile on the login route to slow down credential-stuffing.
text.zh: 一定要走 HTTPS(Secure 旗標需要它)、cookie 保持 HttpOnly 讓 JavaScript 讀不到、使用真正的密碼 KDF,並在登入路由加上速率限制和 Turnstile,拖慢撞庫攻擊(credential stuffing)。

text.en: Never trust by default; verify identity on every request, inside or outside the network. Access is built on this idea.
text.zh: 預設誰都不信任;每個請求都驗證身分,不管在網路內外。Access 就建立在這個概念上。

text.en: Server-side proof that a user already logged in. Stored in KV here, identified by a random id.
text.zh: 伺服器端「這位使用者已經登入過」的憑證。這裡存在 KV,用一個隨機 id 來辨識。

text.en: A small value the browser stores and auto-resends to your domain. We put only the session id in it.
text.zh: 瀏覽器儲存、並會自動回送到你網域的一小段值。我們只在裡面放 session id。

text.en: A signed, self-contained token that carries claims (like the user's email). Access issues one after SSO.
text.zh: 一個已簽章、自帶內容的權杖,裡面裝著聲明(claims,例如使用者 email)。Access 在 SSO 後會發一個。

text.en: Cookie flags: HttpOnly hides it from JavaScript (anti-XSS); Secure sends it only over HTTPS.
text.zh: Cookie 旗標:HttpOnly 讓 JavaScript 讀不到它(防 XSS);Secure 只在 HTTPS 下傳送。

text.en: Controls whether the cookie rides along on cross-site requests. Lax is a sensible default against CSRF.
text.zh: 控制 cookie 是否跟著跨站請求一起送出。Lax 是對抗 CSRF 的合理預設值。

text.en: A slow, salted one-way function (Argon2/bcrypt/scrypt) for storing passwords safely. Not plain SHA-256.
text.zh: 一種慢、加鹽的單向函式(Argon2／bcrypt／scrypt),用來安全地保存密碼。不是單純的 SHA-256。

text.en: Log in once with one identity provider and gain access to multiple apps. That's the Access experience.
text.zh: 用一個身分提供者登入一次,就能進入多個應用程式。這就是 Access 的體驗。

title.en: Never store passwords or user data in the cookie
title.zh: 千萬別把密碼或使用者資料放進 cookie

text.en: The cookie should carry only an opaque session id. Anything you put in a plain cookie is visible and tamperable by the user; keep the real data in KV on the server.
text.zh: cookie 只應該帶一個不透明的 session id。任何放進一般 cookie 的東西,使用者都看得到也能竄改;真正的資料要放在伺服器端的 KV。

en: KV is eventually consistent: a brand-new session is readable almost immediately, but allow a moment for global propagation in edge cases.
Set expirationTtl on every session so stale sessions clean themselves up; mirror it in the cookie Max-Age.
Cloudflare Zero Trust (Access) is free for up to 50 users — perfect for internal tools.
KV pricing is per read/write/delete with a generous free tier; one session lookup per request is cheap.
Add Turnstile to the login form to blunt brute-force and credential-stuffing attacks.
On logout, delete the KV key AND send a Max-Age=0 cookie so both sides forget the session.
zh: KV 是最終一致性(eventually consistent):剛建立的 session 幾乎能立刻讀到,但極端情況下要留一點全球同步的時間。
每個 session 都設 expirationTtl,讓過期的 session 自己清掉;cookie 的 Max-Age 也設成一樣。
Cloudflare Zero Trust(Access)最多 50 位使用者免費 —— 內部工具超適合。
KV 以讀／寫／刪次數計費,且有大方的免費額度;每個請求查一次 session 很便宜。
在登入表單加上 Turnstile,削弱暴力破解和撞庫攻擊。
登出時,要刪掉 KV key「並且」回送一個 Max-Age=0 的 cookie,讓兩邊都忘掉這個 session。

text.en: Internal? Reach for Access first. Public sign-up? Own your sessions in KV. Either way, let Cloudflare's edge do the heavy lifting.
text.zh: 內部用?先想到 Access。對外註冊?自己用 KV 掌管 session。不論哪種,都讓 Cloudflare 的邊緣幫你扛重活。
